dataSources: `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session`, CIDR lookup `ba_ip_ranges` in `transforms.conf` (`vendor_name`, `baa_id`) or `ba_ip_ranges.csv` with one row per approved egress IP
app: Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
spl:
index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" earliest=-7d PublishedApplication="*Epic*BAA*"
| lookup ba_ip_ranges client_ip OUTPUT vendor_name, baa_id
| where isnull(vendor_name)
| iplocation client_ip
| stats count dc(UserName) as distinct_users by client_ip, Country, Org, PublishedApplication
| sort - count
description: Surfaces new source ASNs or IP ranges used by business associates connecting into hosted EHR jump boxes so BA access stays within contracted technical boundaries.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes citrix and sourcetypes citrix:session, citrix:session`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Define `ba_ip_ranges` in `transforms.conf` as a CIDR lookup on `client_ip`, or maintain one row per approved egress IP in `ba_ip_ranges.csv`; (2) refresh when BA contracts change VPN concentrators; (3) alert on first-seen IP before allowlisting; (4) optional threat intel enrichment on `client_ip`. Indexes required: `index=citrix`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=citrix:session`, `sourcetype=citrix:session``.
visualization: Table (client IP, users, sessions), Map (geo), Single value (sessions from non-allowlisted BA egress). Key fields: `Country`, `Org`, `PublishedApplication`, `client_ip`, `distinct_users`.

grandmaExplanation: We watch for surfaces new source asns or ip ranges used by business associates connecting into hosted ehr jump boxes so ba access stays within contracted technical boundaries. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session`, CIDR lookup `ba_ip_ranges` in `transforms.conf` (`vendor_name`, `baa_id`) or `ba_ip_ranges.csv` with one row per approved egress IP.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Define `ba_ip_ranges` in `transforms.conf` as a CIDR lookup on `client_ip`, or maintain one row per approved egress IP in `ba_ip_ranges.csv`; (2) refresh when BA contracts change VPN concentrators; (3) alert on first-seen IP before allowlisting; (4) optional threat intel enrichment on `client_ip`.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" earliest=-7d PublishedApplication="*Epic*BAA*"
| lookup ba_ip_ranges client_ip OUTPUT vendor_name, baa_id
| where isnull(vendor_name)
| iplocation client_ip
| stats count dc(UserName) as distinct_users by client_ip, Country, Org, PublishedApplication
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Business Associate Access — VPN/SSO Sessions Originating from BA Address Space (§164.308(b) / §164.502(e))** — Surfaces new source ASNs or IP ranges used by business associates connecting into hosted EHR jump boxes so BA access stays within contracted technical boundaries.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session`, CIDR lookup `ba_ip_ranges` in `transforms.conf` (`vendor_name`, `baa_id`) or `ba_ip_ranges.csv` with one row per approved egress IP. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: citrix; **sourcetype**: citrix:session. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=citrix, sourcetype="citrix:session", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(vendor_name)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• `stats` aggregates events by **client_ip, Country, Org, PublishedApplication**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: Country, Org, PublishedApplication, client_ip, distinct_users.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (client IP, users, sessions), Map (geo), Single value (sessions from non-allowlisted BA egress).
